1.14: A firm may act if a member of the person's immediate family is the seller provided a different partner is involved
There must be no conflict of interest
The acting conveyancer cannot be the seller or a member of the seller's immediate family
1.15: A firm may act if a member of the person's immediate family is the borrower provided a different partner is involved
There must be no conflict of interest
The acting conveyancer cannot be the borrower or a member of the borrower's immediate family
3.1.3: Notification of seller's solicitor's name and address is only required if specifically requested
3.1.4: Notify Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if the seller is unrepresented to determine if the lender will proceed
3.1.5: Acceptable photographic ID for UK nationals includes valid signed UK Passport, unexpired UK Photo card Driving Licence (Full or Provisional), UK firearms certificate, or UK Blue Disabled Drivers Pass
UK nationals may use an unexpired paper driving licence as non-photographic ID
Non-UK nationals must provide a valid passport with at least six months remaining on the visa, or an unexpired EU/EEA/Swiss ID/Driving Licence

Proof of address documents include utility bills (excluding mobile/broadband) less than 3 months old, council tax demand for the current year, or bank statements less than 3 months old (online copies not accepted)
Identification must be certified by a specified professional (e.g., bank official, teacher, lawyer, Post Office)
Certifiers cannot have a personal or familial relationship with the customer
All ID pages must be certified with specific wording: 'I certify that this is a true copy of the original document' and 'true likeness to the applicant' for photo IDs
Certificates must be dated within the last three months
3.2.3: Notification of seller's solicitor details is only required upon specific request
3.2.4: Notify Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if the seller has no legal representation
4.5a: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk for re-inspections
4.5b: Send Certificate of Title to mortgagecompletions@metrobank.plc.uk
Minimum 5 working days notice required for Certificate of Title submission
4.1: No valuation report is provided to the conveyancer or the customer
4.3: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding incorrect valuer assumptions
5.1.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months
5.2.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if the seller is not the registered proprietor
5.4.4: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding restrictions affecting value or marketability
5.4.5: Registration with the Property Codes Compliance Board Search Code is recommended
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for purchases and remortgages
The policy sum must at least equal the mortgage advance
The policy must be assignable and arranged by the solicitor acting for the lender
5.5.3a: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if planning conditions will not be satisfied by completion
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission is not required
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents is not required
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are accepted if they constitute less than 20% of the floor area and the surveyor confirms suitability
5.7.1c: Report flying freeholds to Mortgage Operations Helpdesk
5.8.1: Security comprising a building converted into no more than four flats where the borrower owns the freehold is not accepted
5.8.5: Security involving cross-over freehold reversions between two leasehold flats is not accepted
5.10.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding restrictions on use
5.13.1: Borrower must provide balance of purchase price from personal funds
Gifts are only accepted from close family members (parents, siblings, grandparents, children, and equivalents by marriage or adoption)
Donors must provide a confirmation that the gift is non-repayable and they hold no interest in the property
Donors residing in the property must sign a Declaration by Occupier
Details of gift amount, donor relationship, source, and accrual must be emailed to mortgagecompletions@metrobank.plc.uk
5.14.15a: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk for absentee or insolvent landlords
5.14.15b: Refer to Mortgage Operations Helpdesk for approval of indemnity insurance for absent landlords
5.14.17a: Refer to special conditions in the mortgage offer for documentation requirements
5.14.17b: Refer to special conditions in the mortgage offer for building safety instructions
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term for second-hand property is 85 years
Minimum unexpired lease term for new build property is 125 years
5.14.9: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk for lease matters
5.14.10: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk for service charge matters
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms may be considered if the conveyancer explains the issue in writing to the lender first
5.14.12: Clear ground rent or service charge receipts are not required
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service is not required
5.15.2a: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding management company issues
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates are not required
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms are not required
5.15.2d: Management company's memorandum and articles of association are not required
5.16.2: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if search entries cannot be certified as unrelated
5.17.5a: Certified copy of power of attorney must be sent to mortgagecompletions@metrobank.plc.uk
5.17.5b: Certified copy of statutory declaration of non-revocation must be sent to mortgagecompletions@metrobank.plc.uk
5.19.1: Lending is not available for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership
5.20.1: Solar panel leases are accepted if the solicitor confirms the provider is MCS accredited and UK Finance conditions are met
Unsatisfied solar panel conditions must be queried via mortgagecompletions@metrobank.plc.uk
5.20.3: Solar panel requirements are the same as 5.20.1
5.20.4: Details of existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed to Mortgage Operations Helpdesk
6.1.3: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if the borrower does not take up the offer
6.2.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding property description discrepancies
6.3.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding purchase price issues
6.4.4: Report all incentives to Mortgage Operations Helpdesk
6.4.5: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if the firm will not control all purchase money
6.5.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if vacant possession is not provided
6.6.1: Buy-to-let properties must be subject to an Assured Shorthold Tenancy (AST) under the Housing Act 1988
Tenancy terms must not breach headleases or covenants
Notice of the lender's right to recover possession under Ground 2 of Schedule 2 to the Housing Act 1988 must be served
6.6.2: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if letting details are unknown at completion
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements should be held on file and not sent to the lender unless requested
6.6.4: Lending on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) is not accepted
6.7.1: Acceptable warranty schemes include NHBC, LABC, Premier Guarantee, Build Zone, Checkmate Castle, Protek, ABC+, and others listed in the section
6.7.2: Warranty documentation should be held on file and not sent to the lender
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes should be held on file
6.7.4: Lending will not proceed without a new home warranty scheme
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificates are not accepted
6.8.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if there is no agreement or bond for unadopted roads/sewers
6.9.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if necessary easements are missing
6.10.2: Retentions are released to the borrower's solicitor
Retentions will not be released if the borrower is in default or arrears
6.11.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding redevelopment or road proposals
6.12.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding pre-emption rights or resale restrictions
6.13.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding improvement or repair grants
7.3: Consent to mortgage is required from all occupants aged 17 or over and must be retained on file
7.4: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk if accuracy of disclosed information is doubted
8.1: Specified third parties must receive independent legal advice and cannot be advised by the firm
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies should be held on file and not sent to the lender
9.2: Indemnity insurance must cover the open market value of the property
10.2b: Minimum of 5 working days notice is required for payment of the advance
10.3: Standard deduction of £35 for CHAPS fee
10.7: On delayed completion, return funds electronically within one working day
10.9: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk regarding delayed completion
10.10: Advance can be held for one working day before it must be returned
10.11: Interest is charged at the mortgage offer rate from receipt until the funds are returned
12.3.1: Contact Mortgage Operations Helpdesk for release of retentions
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is not required as the lender uses an all-monies charge
14.1.5: Original mortgage deeds should be lodged with the Land Registry for paper applications
If using e-Document Registration, the firm must retain the original mortgage deed
14.2.1: Title deeds and documents should be retained by the firm and provided only upon request
14.2.2: No documents are required to be sent after completion
